title: Operation Ultimatum: US/IDF launch coordinated strikes on Iran
detail: B-2 Spirit (509th BW) from Diego Garcia deliver GBU-57 MOP bunker busters on Fordow underground enrichment facility. F-35I Adir strike packages hit Natanz, Isfahan, Parchin. 150+ Tomahawk TLAM launched from DDGs in Persian Gulf. IRGC command nodes targeted across western Iran.

title: Iran retaliates: first ballistic missile wave at Israel
detail: 80+ ballistic missiles including Shahab-3, Emad, and first combat use of Fattah-1 hypersonic MRBM. Arrow-3 and Arrow-2 multi-layer defense activated. Initial intercept rate ~80%. Several impacts in Negev desert and one near Nevatim.

title: US carrier F/A-18s begin SEAD missions over Iran
detail: CVN-69 Eisenhower launches F/A-18E/F Super Hornets with AGM-88 HARM for Suppression of Enemy Air Defenses. S-300PMU2 sites in Tehran and Isfahan targeted. EA-18G Growler jamming support.

title: Oil prices surge 25%+ — Brent crude hits $122
detail: Strait of Hormuz tension spikes markets. Brent crude jumps from $97 to $122/barrel within hours. WTI follows at $118. LNG prices spike 35%. War risk premium on shipping triples. Defense sector stocks: RTX +12%, LMT +14%, NOC +11%.

title: IDF second strike wave: F-35I deep strike on IRGC Aerospace HQ
detail: F-35I Adir strike packages egress Iranian airspace via northern route. IRGC Aerospace Force command center in Tehran suburbs targeted with Rampage hypersonic air-to-ground missiles. Air defense engagement of IDF aircraft reported — at least one F-35I sustained damage, crew safe.

title: UN Security Council emergency session — resolution blocked
detail: Emergency UNSC session convened. US and UK support Israeli right to self-defense. France calls for immediate ceasefire. Russia and China veto condemnation resolution. Arab League emergency meeting announced for 01 March.

title: Global airlines close Iranian airspace — massive rerouting
detail: Emirates, Qatar Airways, Turkish Airlines, Lufthansa, Air France, ITA Airways announce immediate airspace closures over Iran and Iraq. Flights Europe-Asia adding 3-5 hours via Central Asia. Aviation industry estimates $400M/day in additional costs. El Al operating with enhanced security protocols.

title: Iran 2nd wave: 120+ missiles and Shahed-136 drone swarm
detail: Second major strike: 50 Shahed-136 kamikaze drones + 70 ballistic missiles including Sejjil solid-fuel MRBMs. Multi-layer defense activated: Iron Dome intercepts drones, David's Sling engages medium-range, Arrow handles ballistic. 85%+ intercept rate. Drone debris causes fires in northern Negev.

title: SIGINT: IRGC Quds Force-Hezbollah coordination comms detected
detail: US intelligence intercepts IRGC Quds Force coordination with Hezbollah leadership in Beirut. Assessed preparation for second front activation in South Lebanon. IDF Northern Command placed on highest alert. Evacuations of northern Israeli communities accelerated.

title: Emergency sanctions package: US, EU, UK, Canada, Australia
detail: Immediate emergency sanctions on Iran: full banking sector exclusion, oil export ban enforcement, SWIFT network exclusion extended, tech transfer prohibition, aviation parts ban. Secondary sanctions threatened against any country purchasing Iranian oil.

title: B-52H from Al Udeid deploy JASSM-ER against IRGC infrastructure
detail: B-52H Stratofortress from Al Udeid AB Qatar launch AGM-158B JASSM-ER at standoff range, targeting IRGC Revolutionary Guard bases in Khuzestan and Kermanshah provinces. 80+ missiles fired.

title: Hezbollah opens second front: first rocket barrage at northern Israel
detail: Hezbollah activates second front as threatened. ~60 Fajr-5 rockets and Zelzal-2 fired from South Lebanon toward Kiryat Shmona, Haifa, and Tiberias. Iron Dome intercepts ~85%. Kiryat Shmona hospital parking lot struck, 3 wounded. IDF Northern Command activates emergency protocols.

title: IDF strikes Hezbollah Bekaa Valley launch infrastructure
detail: F-16I Sufa and F-15I Ra'am strike Hezbollah Fateh-110 missile depot in Bekaa Valley. Ammunition depot secondary explosions visible on satellite imagery. IDF ground forces move to forward staging positions on northern border.

title: UN OCHA: civilian casualties mounting across theaters
detail: UN Office for Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs reports: Iran civilian casualties est. 800+, Israel 120+ (inc. IDF soldiers), Lebanon 45+. WHO warns of hospital capacity issues in Tel Aviv region. ICRC requests humanitarian corridors.

title: G7 emergency statement — support for US/Israel self-defense
detail: G7 leaders issue joint statement affirming right of self-defense against Iranian aggression. UK commits additional Eurofighter Typhoons to Cyprus. Germany activates NATO Article 4 consultations. France maintains call for ceasefire but will not block NATO support.

title: Houthis launch cruise missiles at USS Gravely — SM-2 intercept
detail: Ansar Allah (Houthis) launch Toofan anti-ship cruise missiles at USS Gravely (DDG-107) in Red Sea. SM-2 Block IIIC intercept successful. No damage to vessel. US Navy retaliatory strike on Houthi launch sites in Hudaydah province announced.

title: Iran 3rd wave — Fattah-1 hypersonics target IDF airbases directly
detail: Third major Iranian strike focuses specifically on IDF airfields: Nevatim, Hatzerim, Ramat David. 12 Fattah-1 hypersonic MRBMs used exclusively — hardest to intercept. Arrow-3 intercepts 8 of 12. 4 Fattah-1 impact near Nevatim, damaging one runway section and destroying 2 aircraft on ground.

title: Planet Labs imagery confirms Natanz 60-70% surface destruction
detail: Commercial satellite imagery from Planet Labs and Maxar Technologies confirms extensive damage to Natanz uranium enrichment facility. Above-ground centrifuge halls A and B assessed destroyed. Underground FEP status cannot be confirmed from imagery. IAEA requests emergency access — Iran refuses.

title: Iran formally threatens complete Strait of Hormuz closure
detail: Iranian Supreme National Security Council issues formal statement threatening complete closure of Strait of Hormuz if attacks continue. IRGC Navy deploys fast attack craft (Seraj-class) and Islamic Republic of Iran Navy mine-laying vessels to Strait entrance. US 5th Fleet places all assets on REDCON-2.

title: US Navy deploys MCM assets to Strait of Hormuz
detail: USS Devastator (MCM-6) and USS Chief (MCM-14) minesweepers ordered to Strait of Hormuz. P-8 Poseidon anti-submarine patrol aircraft increased. USS Gerald R. Ford CSG ordered from Norfolk — ETA 12-14 days. Freedom of Navigation operations continue.

title: UN OCHA updates: 2,400+ total casualties across all theaters
detail: Updated casualty estimates: Iran 1,800+ (including 600+ military, 1,200+ civilian), Israel 340+ (220 IDF, 120 civilian), Lebanon 180+, Yemen 80+. Jordan hosts 12,000 new refugees from Syria/Lebanon. Red Cross establishes emergency coordination centers in Amman and Nicosia.

title: IDF confirms Hezbollah Fateh-110 stockpile reduced 40%
detail: IDF Military Intelligence assessment: Hezbollah Fateh-110 precision missile stockpile reduced approximately 40% through air strikes. However, Katyusha and Fajr-5 short-range rockets largely intact. Approximately 120,000-150,000 rockets remain in Hezbollah's arsenal.

title: Day 3 Hezbollah offensive: 185+ projectiles, Iron Dome under strain
detail: Hezbollah intensifies — 185 total projectiles fired today (Fajr-5, Fateh-110, Zelzal-2, Katyusha salvos). Iron Dome intercept rate 80%. David's Sling activated for Fateh-110 interception. IDF Chief of Staff warns of imminent ground operation in South Lebanon if launches continue.

title: China proposes 72-hour humanitarian pause — US rejects
detail: China's Foreign Minister proposes 72-hour humanitarian pause at UN. US rejects, calling it 'cover for Iran to reconstitute forces.' Qatar and Oman signal Iran may be receptive to back-channel negotiations. French President calls Tel Aviv directly to discuss ceasefire pathway.
